titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL L.F.C. Ch. xxx.5

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=622 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:21:58.484687+00 | translation moa=623 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:22:00.551018+00

div.p:
. W’. dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comitibus . Baronibus . Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis Hominibus Tocius
terre sue Clericis et Laicis  Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri . Me dedisse et Concessisse . et Hac Carta mea Confirmasse .
Iohanni Waler’ . Pro Homagio et seruicio suo totam terram illam quam Willelmus Carpentarius tenuit in Ballebotlia . Scilicet Quintam
Reiam . tocius medietatis de Ballebotlia . Et in Campis de Dreinin  illam terram quam Hugo Giffard’ . et Alexander . de Sancto
Martino . et Godefridus Marescallus . et Winemerus . et alii probi Homines precepto Ade Comitisse Matris mee Rogero . de Camera perambula
uerunt . Et preterea totam terram in Ardarie quam Willelmus . de Beauuer tenuit . Scilicet . terram illam que est ex orientali parte
Riuuli qui currit iuxta terram Galfridi . Capellani . usque ad fontem illum uersus Karel  qui Scottice Tolari nuncupatur . Et totam
terram illam per easdem diuisas quas Galfridus de Maleuille . et Hugo . Giffard’ . et Godefridus . Marescallus . et Winemerus et alii pro
bi homines predicto Willelmo ex omni parte uersus moram et alibi perambulauerunt . Et communem pasturam de Karel . Et
unum Toftum in Karel . illud Scilicet quod . A . Comitissa mater mea predicto Willelmo dedit . Quare uolo et firmiter precipio ut
predictus Iohannes et heredes sui . omnes prescriptas Tenuras habeant et teneant de me et heredibus meis in feudo et hereditate . in
pratis et Pascuis . in Moris et Marresiis . et in Omnibus aliis aisiamentis que ad predictas terras iuste pertinent  Libere et Quiete . Ple
narie et Honorifice . per seruicium unius seruientis in equo cum Halbergello . et faciendo in auxiliis et forinsecis seruiciis
pro tota terra quam habet in Ardarie . tantum seruicii  quantum pertinet ad dimidiam . Carrucatam terre scotticam in Karelis
schire . Concessi etiam predicto Iohanni et heredibus suis . ut Quieti sint de Multura de propria domo sua ad Molendinum meum . Set
ut homines sui dent multuram . Et quod Bladus ipsius Iohannis et heredum suorum molatur ad molendinum meum primo post Bladum qui in
tremello fuerit . nisi Bladus meus ibi sit aut superueniat . Testibus . Philippo . de Valoniis . Camerario . meo . Willelmo Capellano . meo . Willelmo
Cumin . Hugone . et Willelmo et Adam Clericis meis . Magistro Nicholao . et Magistro Martino Clericis meis . Alexandro . Vicecomite . de Striuelin
Willelmo de Valoniis . Alexandro . filio . Thore . Iohanne . de Striuelin . Roberto . Quarrantel’ . Apud Clacmanan . xiii . die Octobris .

William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all the worthy men of the whole of his land, clerk and lay, greeting. Let present and future know me to have given and conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to John Waleram, in return for his homage and service, the whole land which William Carpenter held in Babbet, that is, every fifth rig of the whole half of Babbet, and in the fields of Drony, the land which Hugh Gifford, Alexander de St-Martin, Godfrey the marischal, Winemar, and other worthy men perambulated for Roger de Camera at the instruction of the Countess Ada, my mother; and in addition, the whole land in Airdrie [in Crail] that William de Beauver held, that is, the land which is on the east side of the burn that runs beside the land of Geoffrey the chaplain, as far as the well at Crail which in Gaelic is called 'Tolari' [Toldrie, in Crail]; and the whole land by the same boundaries which Geoffrey de Malville, Hugh Gifford, Godfre the marischal, Winemar and other worthy men perambulated for the aforesaid William on every side towards the moor and elsewhere; and common pasture at Crail; and one toft in Crail, that is, the one which the Countess Ada, my mother, gave to the aforesaid William. Wherefore I will and firmly enjoin that the aforesaid John and his heirs have and hold all the aforewritten holdings of me and my heirs in feu and heritage, in meadows and pastures, in moors and marshes, and in all other easements that belong by right to the aforesaid lands, freely and quitly, fully and honourably, by the service of one sergeand on a horse with a haubergel, and performing in aids and forinsec services in return for the whole land that he has in Airdrie as much service as pertains to half a Scottish ploughgate in Crailshire. I have also granted to the aforesaid John and his heirs that they be quit of multure for their own house at my mill, but that his men should give multure; and that the corn of John and his heirs be milled at my mill first after the corn that is in already in the hopper, unless my corn already be there or arrive in the meantime. As witnesses: Philip de Valognes, my chamberlain; William, my chaplain; William Comyn; Hugh and William and Adam, my clerks; Master Nicholas and Master Martin, my clerks; Alexander, sheriff of Stirling; William de Valognes; Alexander, son of Thor; John of Stirling; Robert Quarrantilly. At Clackmannan, on the 13th day of October.
